cpSummary: Overwhelmingly parents (mostly mothers) treating school-age daughters with long/thick hair after back-to-school lice outbreaks. Buyers are stressed, budget-conscious, and have usually tried 2-3 failed products (pharmacy or natural remedies) before landing on the winner.

usageScenarios.label: Back-to-school infestation (maternelle/primaire)
usageScenarios.reason: Most reviews mention lice caught at school, often in September or after holidays

usageScenarios.label: Repeat infestation after failed pharmacy products
usageScenarios.reason: Buyers arrive having burned through 2-3 other brands first

usageScenarios.label: Whole-family treatment (mother + siblings)
usageScenarios.reason: One bottle rarely enough — parents treat themselves and all children simultaneously

usageScenarios.label: Long/thick/curly/afro hair challenge
usageScenarios.reason: Texture of hair drives product choice and quantity needed

usageScenarios.label: Preventive application
usageScenarios.reason: A subset use it proactively when outbreak announced at school

csSummary: Two sharply divided camps: enthusiasts who call it 'miraculous in one application' and frustrated buyers who report live lice after 8-12 hours of exposure — suggesting real variability in hair-coverage technique, resistance, and expectation management around nits vs lice.

negativeTopics.label: Product ineffective — live lice after treatment
negativeTopics.reason: Dominant 1-2★ complaint: lice still alive after full exposure time
bullets: Live lice found with comb after 8-12h overnight pose
Entire family retreated with zero result
quotes: "After 12h of exposure, lice are still alive — first time this has ever happened with an anti-lice treatment."
"I've done 3 treatments in a week — still finding live lice. 15 euros in the bin."

negativeTopics.label: Nits (lentes) survive — requires 2nd application
negativeTopics.reason: Even positive reviewers note a second round 7 days later is needed
bullets: Product kills adults but nits hatch days later
'One application' marketing claim contradicted by reality
quotes: "The lice die but the nits are another story — they come back a week later."
"One application is not enough despite what the box says."

negativeTopics.label: Cheap plastic comb — inadequate
negativeTopics.reason: Universal gripe: the included comb is flimsy, teeth too short/wide, breaks easily
bullets: Plastic comb breaks on first use
Teeth too short for long/thick hair
Users advise buying separate metal comb
quotes: "The comb is plastic, fragile, better to invest in a metal one."
"Comb broke at the first use — useless on thick hair."

negativeTopics.label: Too greasy — hard to rinse out
negativeTopics.reason: Silicone/oil base requires 2-3 shampoos to remove, leaves hair oily
quotes: "Product is like oil, I had to do 3 shampoos to get it out."
"Very greasy, protect floor and clothes during application."

negativeTopics.label: Irritation — burning eyes, scalp sensitivity
negativeTopics.reason: Spray drift into eyes, scalp reactions reported
quotes: "The product literally burned my son's eyes — he screamed."
"Scalp sensitive for a week after, even without lice."

negativeTopics.label: Too expensive / small bottle
negativeTopics.reason: Price perceived as high relative to pharmacy, bottle too small for families
bullets: 150ml not enough for long hair × multiple family members
quotes: "150ml is tiny when you have long hair and a whole family to treat."
"A real budget when you have kids in nursery school."

negativeTopics.label: Damaged / opened on arrival
negativeTopics.reason: Multiple reports of broken bottles, missing combs, or crystals clogging nozzle
bullets: Crystals formed in lotion block nozzle
quotes: "Bottle arrived open, product spilled everywhere."
"Full of white crystals, impossible to use properly."

negativeTopics.label: Overnight application too constraining
negativeTopics.reason: Some formats require 8+ hour overnight pose — hard on young kids
bullets: Kids can't sit still overnight with product
Rinsing in morning disrupts school routine
quotes: "Leaving it on a child's head for 8 hours is really constraining."
"Impossible to make a 3-year-old keep it on all night."

negativeInsights.finding: Marketing promises 'effective in one application / 5 minutes' but nits require 7-10 days to hatch and second treatment.
negativeInsights.implication: Expectation mismatch drives the majority of 1-2★ reviews — buyers feel deceived even when the product partially worked.

negativeInsights.finding: Included plastic comb is universally rated inferior to metal combs.
negativeInsights.implication: Every brand that bundles a plastic comb gets the same complaint — opportunity to differentiate with proper stainless steel comb.

negativeInsights.finding: Silicone/oil spray drift causes significant eye irritation, especially on young children.
negativeInsights.implication: Safety-label failure risk; claims of 'suitable from 6 months' contradicted by painful user reports.

negativeInsights.finding: 150ml bottles insufficient for multi-child families with long hair.
negativeInsights.implication: Family-size (400-500ml) SKU with better price-per-ml would capture repeat buyers.

negativeInsights.finding: 'Satisfait ou rembourse' 3-day refund window is seen as fraudulent — shorter than the treatment cycle.
negativeInsights.implication: Refund policy shorter than treatment cycle generates repeat 1★ reviews citing bad faith.

negativeInsights.finding: Puressentiel and natural-formula products under-deliver on heavy infestations.
negativeInsights.implication: Buyers who started natural end up returning to dimethicone-based products — positioning the natural product as 'prevention only' would set correct expectations.

positiveTopics.label: Effective in one application
positiveTopics.reason: Dominant 5★ theme: kills lice and nits in single overnight or 5-min application
bullets: Dead lice visible on comb right after rinsing
Saves weeks of fighting with other products
quotes: "One application and goodbye lice and nits — I recommend 100%."
"After months of fighting, this product saved us in one use."

positiveTopics.label: Best after trying many alternatives
positiveTopics.reason: Users explicitly compare against 3-5 other failed products from pharmacy
bullets: Finally found 'the one' after long battle
quotes: "After trying Marie Rose, Paranix, Apaisyl, essential oils — this is the only one that worked."
"The only truly effective anti-lice product I've found."

positiveTopics.label: Easy spray application
positiveTopics.reason: Spray nozzle praised as faster/cleaner than bottle-pour lotions
bullets: No waste compared to bottle formats
Quick to apply on squirming kids
quotes: "Much easier than bottle-format products — just pchit and done."
"Spray lets you dose precisely without waste."

positiveTopics.label: Short 5-minute exposure time
positiveTopics.reason: Compared favorably to competitors requiring 15-30min or overnight
bullets: No need to sleep with product
quotes: "5 min is top — works great, I recommend!"
"Much easier than overnight products for young kids."

positiveTopics.label: Cheaper than pharmacy
positiveTopics.reason: Amazon price routinely cited as half the pharmacy price
quotes: "100% effective and half the price of pharmacy."
"Hard to find in pharmacy, so grateful Amazon sells it."

positiveTopics.label: No harsh chemicals / family-safe
positiveTopics.reason: Dimethicone or essential-oil formulas praised as gentle for kids from 6 months
bullets: Suitable for toddlers and pregnant women
quotes: "Formulated without neurotoxic insecticide, acts mechanically."
"Suitable for kids from 6 months and adults — finally a safe option."

positiveTopics.label: Pleasant / neutral smell
positiveTopics.reason: Unlike vinegar or older-generation products, no chemical stench
quotes: "No unpleasant smell, kids didn't complain."
"Smells fine, not the chemical horror of other brands."

positiveTopics.label: Hair stays healthy after treatment
positiveTopics.reason: Oil base doubles as conditioner, hair soft after rinsing
quotes: "Hair is silky after, like a conditioner."
"Doesn't damage hair, good for long afro-textured hair."

positiveInsights.finding: Biggest advocates are repeat-customer parents who bought after 3-5 failed attempts with other brands.
positiveInsights.implication: Marketing angle: 'the last anti-lice product you'll ever buy' — position as graduation from failed pharmacy alternatives.

positiveInsights.finding: 5-minute pose time is the #1 differentiator called out vs overnight competitors.
positiveInsights.implication: Fast-pose format commands premium and drives 5★ reviews from stressed parents.

positiveInsights.finding: Dimethicone formulas consistently praised for physical (non-insecticide) action.
positiveInsights.implication: 'No pesticides / safe for 6m+' is a durable positioning hook as buyers grow anti-chemical.

positiveInsights.finding: Amazon price consistently 40-50% below pharmacy — key purchase driver.
positiveInsights.implication: Losing Amazon channel would collapse the category; pharmacy brands must decide whether to own DTC or cede to e-commerce.

positiveInsights.finding: Hair-conditioning side-effect (softness, detangling) appears as unexpected delight.
positiveInsights.implication: Lean into 'treatment + hair care' dual-benefit in the packaging copy.

buyersMotivation.reason: Most buyers arrive in panic mode after discovering lice

customerExpectations.reason: Packaging promises drive this expectation — biggest disappointment source

customerExpectations.label: Safe for kids from 6 months+
customerExpectations.reason: Non-irritating, no neurotoxins, no burning eyes

customerExpectations.reason: One 150ml bottle should treat multiple heads

customerExpectations.reason: Users hate oily residue requiring 3 shampoos
